questions.question: Why does the scheduler use UCB scoring instead of always picking the current best branch?
options: Because UCB is faster to compute
Because greedy picks never explore; UCB balances exploitation with exploration
Because asyncio requires UCB
Because the queue cannot be sorted otherwise
questions.explanation: Greedy locks onto the first lead. Uniform never exploits. UCB picks the leader while reserving capacity for less-explored branches via the sqrt(ln N / n) term.
questions.question_zh: 为什么 scheduler 用 UCB 打分,而不是总挑当前最佳分支?
options_zh: 因为 UCB 计算更快
因为贪心选取从不探索; UCB 在 exploitation 与 exploration 之间取得平衡
因为 asyncio 要求 UCB
因为否则队列无法排序
questions.explanation_zh: 贪心会锁住第一个领先者。均匀采样从不利用。UCB 在挑领先者的同时,通过 sqrt(ln N / n) 项保留容量给探索较少的分支。

questions.question: What score does a branch with zero completed runs receive under UCB1?
options: Zero
The exploration constant c
Positive infinity, so untried branches are always picked first
The mean reward of all other branches
questions.explanation: The lesson assigns +inf for runs=0. This guarantees every branch is tried at least once before any branch is revisited.
questions.question_zh: 在 UCB1 下,完成次数为 0 的分支得到什么分?
options_zh: exploration 常数 c
正无穷,所以未尝试的分支总会先被挑选
其他分支的均值 reward
questions.explanation_zh: 本节对 runs=0 赋 +inf。这保证每个分支至少被尝试一次,然后才会重新访问任何分支。

questions.question: How does the scheduler keep multiple slots busy?
options: It runs the runner in a thread pool
It uses asyncio.create_task per dispatched hypothesis and awaits FIRST_COMPLETED on the in-flight set
It uses multiprocessing
It blocks the event loop on a sleep
questions.explanation: Each dispatched hypothesis becomes a task. The main loop waits on FIRST_COMPLETED so a finished slot is freed immediately and another hypothesis is dispatched.
questions.question_zh: scheduler 如何让多个 slot 同时忙起来?
options_zh: 用线程池跑 runner
对每条派发的假设用 asyncio.create_task,并对在飞集合用 FIRST_COMPLETED 等待
用 multiprocessing
在 sleep 上阻塞事件循环
questions.explanation_zh: 每条派发的假设变成一个 task。主循环等 FIRST_COMPLETED,完成的 slot 立即被释放,下一个假设随即被派发。

questions.question: When does the scheduler emit a paper.trigger for a branch?
options: Every time a result lands on that branch
On the first result whose branch mean meets or exceeds the paper threshold, and only once per branch
Only at the end of the run
When the prune floor is hit
questions.explanation: The trigger fires once per branch, the first time the branch's mean crosses the threshold. The paper_triggered flag on BranchStats prevents repeats.
questions.question_zh: scheduler 何时为某分支发出 paper.trigger?
options_zh: 该分支每次有结果落地时
在该分支的均值首次达到或超过 paper 阈值的那次结果上,且每个分支只发一次
只在运行结束时
命中 prune 下限时
questions.explanation_zh: trigger 每个分支发一次,在分支均值首次跨过阈值时触发。BranchStats 上的 paper_triggered 标志阻止重复。

questions.question: What happens to a branch whose mean reward stays below the prune floor after the minimum-runs threshold?
options: Nothing; UCB will eventually pick another branch
The branch is marked pruned and remaining hypotheses on that branch are removed from the queue
The scheduler raises an exception
The reward floor is lowered automatically
questions.explanation: Pruning removes the branch from future scheduling. Pruning is separate from the picker: UCB ranks, pruning culls.
questions.question_zh: 如果一个分支在最低运行数阈值后均值仍低于 prune 下限,会怎样?
options_zh: 什么也不发生; UCB 最终会选另一个分支
该分支被标记为 pruned,队列中属于它的剩余假设被移除
scheduler 抛出异常
reward 下限被自动降低
questions.explanation_zh: Pruning 把分支从未来调度中移除。Pruning 与挑选分开: UCB 排序,prune 剔除。

questions.question: Which two budgets does the scheduler enforce as hard limits?
options: Memory and CPU
max_experiments (total runs) and max_seconds (wall clock)
slots and queue depth
paper triggers and prune count
questions.explanation: Both fire as stop reasons. max_experiments caps total work. max_seconds caps wall time. The trace records which one stopped the run.
questions.question_zh: scheduler 强制的两个硬性预算是?
options_zh: 内存与 CPU
max_experiments (总运行数) 与 max_seconds (wall clock)
slot 数与队列深度
paper trigger 数与 prune 数
questions.explanation_zh: 两者都作为 stop reason 触发。max_experiments 限制总工作量,max_seconds 限制墙钟时间。Trace 记录是哪一个停下了运行。
